content:
# Petrogenesis

**Summary**: The origin and formation of rocks.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] analyzed the petrogenesis of the peridotite xenoliths to identify melting and metasomatic events.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] investigated the petrogenesis of northern Midyan terrane granitoids using geochemistry and isotopes.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] investigated the petrogenesis of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton in relation to Arabian Shield A-type granites.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] Used to understand the multi-stage history of the western Arabian plate.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] investigated the petrogenesis of northern Midyan terrane granitoids using geochemistry and isotopes.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] investigated the petrogenesis of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton in relation to Arabian Shield A-type granites.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] Analyzed via mineral chemistry and REE patterns.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Petrogenesis consists of depletion, enrichment, and metasomatic events. ([[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023, p. 102]])


- Petrogenesis was determined by identifying distinct magmatic pulses and slab failure signatures. ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 17]])

- The critical mineral enrichment is primarily magmatic controlled by ligands in the melt for the granite. ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024, p. 16]])
